text.title: styled-components Basics
text.summary: Transient props ($prefix), typed variants via css helper, DefaultTheme augmentation, and ThemeProvider setup for styled-components.

rule.statement: Always prefix styled-components props with $ (e.g., $variant, $size). Plain prop names leak to the DOM and trigger React unknown-prop warnings.
rule.rationale: React passes all non-standard props to the DOM element; styled-components forwards unknown props unless prefixed with $, causing console warnings and invalid HTML attributes.

section.title: DefaultTheme Augmentation

rule.statement: Declare a DefaultTheme augmentation in types/styled.d.ts. Without it, the theme parameter inside template literals is typed any and color values become magic strings.
rule.rationale: The styled-components DefaultTheme is empty by default; augmenting it gives TypeScript the full theme shape, enabling autocomplete and type checking inside styled blocks.

section.reference: DefaultTheme declaration — the augmentation file agents forget

rule.statement: Pick exactly one CSS-in-JS library per app. Never mix styled-components and Emotion in the same bundle.
rule.rationale: Both runtimes are included in the bundle even if only one is actively used; each runtime re-injects its global style sheet, causing unpredictable cascade order.
